dislike_text:
Worst increments 3 to 4 percentage. 7 percentage fir top performer. Also they keep pushing hike by one quarter every year. So if you stay for 4 years you get only 3 hikes.
Very poor office infrastructure. They don't provide monitors, keyboard, mouse etc. for hybrid employees. When we asked, they said how can we spend so much money when you are not going to use it everyday. 
Office facilities are the worst. Bad coffee, bad food vendors. Only junk food available in cafeterias. They don't give a crap about employees well being. 
In hybrid policy you have to be in office 6 hours a day for 3 days a week. That means you will spend both sides in traffic and then go work from home because all the client calls are in the evening. Office day is a 12 hour day by default. If you go late you won't find parking. The cab facility is very limited and not provided for all projects. 
They stopped shift allowance also saying that working till 9pm is minimum expectation.
Most of the projects are staff aug mentation. Regardless of designation there is high chance that you will be doing unsatisfactory, trivial and thankless work that no one cares about. As long as you as you are billable nobody cares if you are realising your potential or not. I know good engineers who are in trainee designation even after 3 years of experience. However they would hire a similar person from outside two levels above them. No standards in hiring. 
I heard Bench policy is 60 days and then you are asked to leave in most cases. Especially if you are senior 
Also, this is the first company I have seen that doesn't even take referrals seriously. If I refer someone in the portal they will never even get an interview even if there are openings. 
A lot of deductions happen from salary. Your own medical insurance, domiciliary, gratuity, even group term insurance is part of your CTC. Parents insurance is super expensive and has significant co payment. At the end, the in hand salary is very less. There is not much benefit of being part of such a big conglomerate. Analyze CTC carefully if you get an offer. 
Variable pay is never paid in full. Don't expect more than 80%
HR team is the worst. Just keep spamming with annoying emails asking to share team photos of days when you come to office. But never answer when you actually need them. I know someone who had a serious surgery and they were not giving him extra 2 weeks WFH which he genuinely needed. 
Talent team is next level. You will be asked to come to office on weekend for hiring without any comp off or overtime just out of the goodness of your heart. It's voluntary but they do try to build pressure and some folks succumb. ... read more

The company's curriculum is so stupid that they take 12 hours class per day and after that give loads of assignments to complete within that day ,so total time infront of laptop would be 14-15 hours per day excluding lunch break. They're behaving as if we are not humans and some kind of machine. Everyday feels like hell there. I mean where is the human rights now? How can someone force you to study and do all the assignments per day by sitting 14-15 hours. No self study time is given, even you would sometimes skip lunch in order to attend classes. I don't think a graduate student should be kept in this schedule from day 1. Atleast give us some time to adapt to corporate world, so that we can gradually increase our time to work, but here everything is forced on you. Even the quality of food is very bad there. Approx 10k would be spend on food if you'll have your 3 time meal in the cafeteria. 6k is the room rent and 1k for laundry. And they provide you 25k stipend. So at last you'll be l eft with 5k in your hand. You'll not get time to talk to your family or relax at the weekend also, because there is 6 day working there and that too on sunday they'll give you extra pressure and extra assignments so that you could not step out of the campus.
Apart from this the campus rules are insane, you can only go out of the campus on sundays by taking permission from HR, if they deny then you can do nothing. You only have to wear collar tshirt and jeans. No trouser and normal tshirts allowed there. You can only wear those in your room. You have to be there in class by 9 sharp, if you're late even 1 minute, they'll take you to the manager for extra pamper(pressure). It doesn't matter if you had breakfast or not, you had lunch or not or dinner. You will sleep max to max 5 hours daily and work 15 hours, this is the reality of LTIM ... read more

like_text:
What I've observed is that to get assigned to a project, you need to stay closely connected with your WFM team and dedicate time to learning. If your profile is proposed to a client, there's usually an internal interview first. These internal interviews can feel like rocket science-they often ask deep technical questions. After that, the client interview can be as challenging as interviewing for a Microsoft and Google developer role.
So, it's essential to prepare thoroughly and have a strong grasp of core concepts if you're a developer. If you commit to focused on preparation for two week-even working through weekends-you'll significantly increase your chances of getting into a project.
I personally lost my first interview, but within three weeks, I was selected by three different clients.

like_text: My experience at LTIMindtree has been highly positive because of the collaborative work culture and the emphasis on continuous learning. The organization provides ample opportunities for skill development through training programs and exposure to cutting-edge work-life balance and employee well-being, which creates an environment where people feel valued and supported. Additionally, the leadship is approachable and encourages innovation, making it a great place to contribute and thrive.
dislike_text: While LTIMindtree offers a great work environment overall, one area that could be improved is the speed of decision-making and reducing process overhead in certain workflows. At times, multiple layers of approvals can slow down execution, especially in fast-paced projects where agility is critical. Steamlining these processes and empowering teams with more automnomy would enhance efficiently and help us respond quicker to client needs.

There is absolutlely no job security. When bench count rises, employees are presurred to resign on calls. Overall, lateral hires should think twice before joining this company

dislike_text:
Despite consistently receiving OP rating and being recognized as a top performer, the increment percentage has remained limited to around 4-5% which feels quite discouraging. Over the past 3 years, the annual appraisal cycle has repeatedly been pushed by one quarter, and this year, for some employee, it has been delayed further by two quarters, that mean if employee worked for 4 years still, they would receive the hike for 3 years, which is very demotivating.
Additionally, the current salary structure is not very employee friendly. A large portion of the compensation is included under CTC, resulting in significant deductions are leaving limited take-home pay. Referral efforts are also not giving due importance, as candidates are not interviewed even when relevant openings exist.
There have been multiple instances where employees have gone above and beyond their regular duties-working weekends and even supporting on national holidays- yet shift allowances are not always honored, citing lack of approvals. At times, employees are also advised not to fill timesheets, which is quite demotivating.
Regarding leave policies, the medical leave requirements are quite stringent. Even in genuine medical situations, employees are asked for extensive documentation, and if the leave is less than three days, it is usually adjusted against paid leave. This started happening after LTI merger, Mindtree never did this kind of injustice with their employees. 
Overall, the work culture reflects a significant level of micromanagement, and interactions with higher management often lack transparent communication and employee centric decision-making. I sincerely hope that these concerns are acknowledged and addressed to build a more supportive and motivating environment for everyone. ... read more

I have joined as senior software Engineer...for .net development and they allocated me to a production support project without my knowledge and when I raised a concern no one from the leadership team responded.... And I shifted to development in the same team with hr help.... But then manager started taunting me for this... And he used to talk unprofessionally and always used to pass unethical statements... Also, the salary expected Is 14 lpa and we completed all the interviews... But in the last minute the day before I join ... They changed the ctc to 11 lpa. 
The working hours at office is 6 hrs and remaining hours we can login after reaching home... But my manager says I should login from only office. And he asks me to stay upto 11:30pm in the night. Which is not necessary per work or per office rules .

like_text: Project Opportunities- Projects are limited, and not all employees get proper assignments. However, I appreciate my project lead, who has been supportive and professional.
dislike_text:
HR Responsiveness- Ms. Manisha Tripaty HR rarely responds to emails or Teams chat. Even after 10+ reminders and looping her managers in CC, responses are either delayed or irrelevant, which causes frustration for employees.
Job Security- if there is no project allocated within 2 months, employees are asked to leave immediately.
WFH Requests- Very first we did not get any reply, and if they do reply, they demand unnecessary proofs as if employees are being cross-examined in a court.
Sick Leave Policy- Even for something as common as a severe headache, employees cannot take sick leave unless an MBBS doctor admits them and issues a certificate recommending 2+ days of rest. The policy is impractical.
Salary Hike- Salary hikes are almost non-exist. In rare cases, employees receive only around 1.67%.

I joined org in Jul. 2022  as cloud engineer and my training also revolved around this position, in oct 2022 i was tagged in a project but unfortunately my project revolved around support through email and call.
The training i took and the work i was doing was miles apart, then after 1 year they introduced chat in the project at this time it became totally BPO project. I was frustrated and desperate to change the project however my manager did not take it seriously and waved it off.
The only reason i continued because of my team members. The chat made everything worst I was not able to get any breaks no time to relax and it became 24/7 projects with rotational shift.
My frustration built like nothing i had before. I was barely making in project. In my notice period HR did not take my exit interview she called me and had only 1 min call, my Sr manager took call with me on teams a week before my LWD and that even of 2 min. No  retention efforts were made no role change or project change offered.
Due this i had to resign without any offers and i did not get any time to learn more skills. I used to get very little time to study.
For me it was very bad experience as a fresher in corporate. ... read more

dislike_text: I rated LTIMindtree low due to several concerns across key areas. The work-life balance was poor, with long, unpredictable hours and little regard for personal time. Salary levels were not competitive, and annual increments were minimal. Promotions lacked transparency and seemed influenced more by internal politics than merit. Job security became a major concern after the merger, with unclear communication and frequent reshuffling. Opportunities for structured skill development were limited, with most learning happening informally on the job. Overall work satisfaction was low, as employee efforts were rarely acknowledged, and the environment was heavily delivery-focused. Additionally, the company culture grew increasingly rigid and hierarchical post-merger, with reduced engagement and openness from leadership.

dislike_text:
I would like to share my feedback regarding the most disappointing experience I had during my time at LTIMindtree. It relates to the annual appraisal process handled by my previous manager, Mr. Mohan Arunagiri.
Despite putting in consistent effort, managing multiple responsibilities across Service Desk, IDAM/IAM, EUB, and SAM, and receiving positive feedback from peers, clients, and stakeholders, I was given an unexpectedly low rating. The feedback comments provided were vague, lacked evidence, and failed to reflect the actual contributions I made throughout the appraisal period.
What made this situation worse was the lack of transparency and opportunity for discussion. My repeated attempts to understand the rationale behind the rating and to present my side were either dismissed or not addressed adequately. This experience left me feeling demotivated, undervalued, and unheard—especially when I had tangible results, project outcomes, and CSAT scores to demonstrate my performance.
I str ongly believe that such practices not only affect individual morale but also go against the core values of fairness and integrity that LTIMindtree stands for. I hope the leadership takes note of this pattern to ensure that appraisal processes in the future are more transparent, evidence-based, and allow a fair channel for employees to represent themselves.
I remain committed to contributing positively to the organization and hope that this feedback will help improve the experience for other associates in similar situations. ... read more

dislike_text:
- Culture isn't good from a corporate standpoint. After the merger, culture went downhill.
- People are not motivated to work - hiring practices are not good, general calibre of people is on the lower end. But for this salary, it's hard to get good candidates.
- I joined in May 2022, and my salary increment has been 5% in the last ~ years (it is Feb 2025 now). Infact in 2023, I got 2% hike even though my annual review rating was 3 out of 4. In 2024, I got a rating of 4 on 4 and my salary increment was 2.5%. 
- They change the annual review cycle to whenever they want. We are seeing appraisal cycle of 15-16 months each because they say they haven't been performing. But that's not true - the CEO doesn't take a pay cut. His earnings keep increasing while we don't get a hike.
- They have way too many people on the bench but the company is also losing projects and accounts left right and centre to other competitors in the market.
- HR and the Workforce Management team don't really care abou t you - they only focus on enforcing policies. Meaning if you've been on the bench for more than 60 days, they will call you and ask you to resign. 
- On projects, I've had to be online and support the US team at midnight/1-2 am even though I'm technically on a day time 9-6 shift. We don't get paid overtime and they just automatically expect us to work and be available at all times. 
- They ask you to come to office all 5 days if you're on the bench. And if you are non compliant, they deduct your leaves. That way when they ask you to resign, they don't have to compensate you for your leaves as part of the severance. ... read more

working at LTIMIndtree Ltd has been an incredibly rewarding experience. The combination of a dynamic work environment, a culture of continuous learning and a commitment to innovation makes it an ideal place to grow.
The support and camaraderie among colleagues foster a sense of belonging and drives me to achieve collective goals. Additionally, the company's dedication to employee's development through various training programs and mentorship opportunities has significantly enhanced my skills and knowledge.
Inclusive approach creates a positive and motivating atmosphere, encouraging us to take ownership of my work and contribute meaningfully to the organizations success.
dislike_text:
Undeterred by my appreciation for the LTIMindtree Ltd company, there are few areas which requires improvement.
First thing to mention is that the salary increments are not as expected. Regular and timely salary reviews and increments are crucial for employee motivation and satisfaction.
There is a lack of timely communication from management when it comes to the annual increments and other important updates. To maintain the transparency and the trust within the company, clear and consistent communication is very much required. This will be beneficial to align with the employee expectations with company policies and procedures.

like_text: stability

Attractive Initial Salary Hike: One of the company's biggest draws is its ability to offer a significant initial salary hike, often higher than what most competitors provide. This makes it an appealing choice or us for us and we fall in this trap
dislike_text:
The company faces significant challenges that negatively impact its work environment and employee satisfaction:
1. Lack of HR Support and Fairness in Non-Billable Work: The HR team often fails to address associates' concerns effectively. Employees on the bench are left to find projects on their own, and if they remain unallocated for more than 60 days, they are pressured to resign. There have also been instances where managers made employees work without billability on tasks during bench period assuring him billability. Those dont count in work, eventually leading to layoffs.
2. Poor Communication from Higher Management: Higher management offers minimal support, and employees must independently resolve issues or seek guidance, making processes inefficient and frustrating.
3. Unsatisfactory Compensation and Career Growth: Pay increments are below industry standards, and promotions are rare. Hard work is seldom recognized or rewarded, discouraging employees from going the extra mile.
4. Limited Project Diversity: The majority of assignments involve support roles or body shopping, with very few full-fledged development opportunities. This limits employees' skill enhancement and career progression.
5. Micromanagement Culture: The company suffers from excessive micromanagement. Even senior management, including directors, often focus on day-to-day operational tasks instead of strategic leadership. Developers frequently face multiple interruptions daily, with repetitive questions like, "What's the status?" and "What have you done?"
Overall Assessment:
The lack of recognition, poor HR practices, limited growth opportunities, and an overbearing micromanagement culture make this company unsuitable for professionals seeking career development or a supportive work environment. Potential candidates should carefully weigh these factors before joining. ... read more

worked at LTIMindtree for over a year and, based on my personal experience, I would not recommend it to those looking for stability or a professional work culture. Here are some key concerns I faced:
Management practices felt biased and inconsistent.
There was a constant sense of job insecurity, with frequent threats of sudden termination.
Upon resignation, employees are often asked to leave on the same day—notice periods are not honored.
HR communication, in my experience, was unprofessional and lacked empathy.
A toxic environment driven by favoritism and internal politics.
Terminations can happen over a phone call without prior warning or discussion.
Most concerning: My relieving letter explicitly mentioned the date resignation requested and  rejection of that and early releaving, 
 which is highly unusual and can negatively impact future job prospects.

dislike_text:
The work culture in the Microsoft CSS project at LTIMindtree is very poor. There is excessive micromanagement, and work-life balance is non-existent. The benefits are lacking, and when it comes to appraisals, the average increase is just 0-2 percent, so there's little room for growth. Promotions are handled internally, without any corresponding salary hike or change in salary grade or designation. Instead, employees receive a simple email from management stating that they will be taking on a new role, such as a Technical Advisor.
If you are a fresher or a developer and receive an offer from LTIMindtree for the Microsoft CSS Technical Support project, I would strongly advise against joining this organization.

However after released from 1st project, within a month, I got selected for new project/account after 2 interview rounds and along with this, outside also I have got 1 offer. I was keen to go outside but LTIMINDTREE director/project manager asked me to revoke the resignation and will give onsite opportunity immediately. After discussion with higher management, I revoked resignation. 
Visa process also started and it took around 60 days. I have got the visa and was about to fly within 4 days. At the end moment, LTIMindtree manager called me and told project which you got selected is on hold, so travel plan has been canceled.
After this situation, was expecting help to get project immediately but no help got from LTIMINDTREE management because of them I revoked resignation and denied outside offer.
Situation is very bad here. Please think 3-4 times before joining LTIMindtree. ... read more

like_text:
Work Life Balance: Flexible Schedules, Reasonable workload and Supportive team practices, made it manageable to stay productive without burning out. 
Skill Development: Good Access to learning - Internal Programs, Certifications, and Diverse project exposure helped build bandwidth and depth. 
Company Culture: Collaborative, Inclusive Culture with approachable leaders, and Cross-Functional teamwork. 
Job Security and Work Satisfaction: Stable environment with meaningful responsibilities and clear business impact.
dislike_text:
Salary and Promotions: Compensation tends to lag market benchmarks, CHRO and CEO Always delay annual hikes by saying some reason on the market condition when it comes to salary hikes annually market conditions are always bad for them. Company growth they will show in thousands of crores.. For Salary Hikes it will be decimals not single digits. You can forget double digit hikes.  
Promotions Cycle or process could be more transparent, consisitent and faster.. as of now no guidelines for promotions.  Clear criteria and timelines would improve trust and motivation. 
In the name of competency development assessment every year for level 4 and 5 employees they will allocate 50% to measure your performance for others this is not the case. one small assessment or 30min interview will decide your 1 year of work for 50% of your appraisal.

The senior Recruiter (POOJA) told me she has never made a JD after joining the firm. Everything they do is too much cluttered. They dont have a proper system  also they usually hire on third party payroll so that they can kick you any time they want. also they want to work after working hours as well sometime till 11PM. you will see recruiter positions open for  LTI every few months becau se people leave after seeing this bad working culture.
